title: How Long Does It Take For Swelling To Go Down After A Procedure？ | Dr Tim's Tips
filename: How Long Does It Take For Swelling To Go Down After A Procedure？ ｜ Dr Tim_s Tips.en.vtt
excerpt: So how long does it take for swelling to go down after a procedure so probably the area that swells the most in the face is the lips and we once surveyed some clinicians who had their lips treated and got some very interesting data it's obviously not a pure scientific study but t…

segments.text: so how long does it take for swelling to go down after a procedure so probably the area that swells the most in the face is the lips and we once surveyed some clinicians who had their lips treated and got some very interesting data it's obviously not a pure scientific study but this survey showed that the majority of people have notable noticeable swelling for two to three days after a procedure and some of them would last for a week and a minority about 10% about 5% would have some subtle swelling up to two weeks afterwards the vast majority within two to three days would say they were carrying on with life is normal without any particular difficulty and that swelling is variable between people it's worse when the weather's warm it's worse with certain people just the products seem to react a little bit I know I certainly have patients who I'm aware swell significantly a degree of swelling that almost looks like a reaction but it's always limited it lasts for a day and then it goes away and these

types of things do go in the mix and some people have almost no swelling at all but I would advise people to expect running guaranteed for two to three days with the average patient having very little 80% gone within a week and then there's at 5% swelling up to two weeks afterwards so I hope that helps

transcript: so how long does it take for swelling to go down after a procedure so probably the area that swells the most in the face is the lips and we once surveyed some clinicians who had their lips treated and got some very interesting data it's obviously not a pure scientific study but this survey showed that the majority of people have notable noticeable swelling for two to three days after a procedure and some of them would last for a week and a minority about 10% about 5% would have some subtle swelling up to two weeks afterwards the vast majority within two to three days would say they were carrying on with life is normal without any particular difficulty and that swelling is variable between people it's worse when the weather's warm it's worse with certain people just the products seem to react a little bit I know I certainly have patients who I'm aware swell significantly a degree of swelling that almost looks like a reaction but it's always limited it lasts for a day and then it goes away and these types of things do go in the mix and some people have almost no swelling at all but I would advise people to expect running guaranteed for two to three days with the average patient having very little 80% gone within a week and then there's at 5% swelling up to two weeks afterwards so I hope that helps
